title: Pulmonary function review

red_flags.text: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped symptom correlation, inhaler or medicine use, smoking, oxygen or examination context, and monitoring fields in asthma.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.5.1–1.6.4 — monitoring asthma control and uncontrolled asthma

examination.text: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped spirometry, bronchodilator-response, respiratory-examination, and objective-test fields in suspected asthma.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma

referral.text: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped adult COPD FEV1, FVC, symptom, smoking, medicine-effect, oxygen, referral-need, and annual monitoring fields.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

source_limitations: The workflow does not require asthma, COPD, adulthood, or a specific pulmonary-function indication.

Acute distress, interpretation of decline, obstruction or restriction, DLCO, respiratory rate, blood gas, chest radiography, generic clinician or medication plans, fixed follow-up ranges, PRN wording, and some examination fields remain unsupported.
Test validity, interpretation, diagnosis, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
